title: Velvety Peruvian Pepper Soup
description: A silky, umami-packed onepot soup brimming with mixed peppers and a touch of toasted bread for crunch, all while staying lowcalorie and vegetarian-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced red, yellow, and orange bell peppers; cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Mix in the tomato paste, smoked paprika, and pinch of sea salt; cook for 1 minute to bloom the flavors.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat and let it cook for 10 minutes to meld the flavors.
Lower the heat again and whisk in the Greek yogurt until the soup is smooth and creamy; continue to simmer for an additional 5 minutes, being careful not to let it boil.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
Ladle the soup into bowls and top each serving with toasted bread cubes (and optionally, a dash of crushed pumpkin seeds).
Serve hot and enjoy the velvety texture and vibrant pepper flavors.
